core_question: Should we invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion as a commercially viable energy source within the next 15 years?
central_thesis: The storyline should be led by the strongest public evidence, then translated into management choices.
narrative_focus: Evidence is densest around: funding, timeline, adoption, technology
structure_logic: Open with the executive decision, show source-backed data exhibits, then move through business implications, risks and action gates.
evidence_must_cover: To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

All 8 awardees are presently working toward presenting pre-conceptual designs and technology roadmaps of their FPP concepts within the first 18 months of the Milestone program—roughly late 2025 (18 months into the Milestone Program).

Department of Energy (DOE) today announced $107 million in funding for six projects in the Fusion Innovative Research Engine (FIRE) Collaboratives , and that several privately funded fusion companies have completed early critical-path science and technology (S&T) milestones in the Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program (“the Milestone Program”).

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: Nuclear fusion will not achieve grid-connected electricity generation before 2040.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, near-term investment in fusion is premature; focus on fission and renewables.
needed_evidence: Published timelines from major fusion projects (ITER, SPARC) and independent assessments.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: The levelized cost of electricity (LCOE) from fusion will be above $50/MWh by 2050, making it uncompetitive with renewables.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, fusion may only serve niche markets; if false, it could disrupt baseload power.
needed_evidence: LCOE estimates from fusion companies and independent studies, compared to solar/wind LCOE projections.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: Private investment in fusion startups exceeds $5 billion cumulatively by 2025, indicating strong commercial interest.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: Validates market confidence; high investment may accelerate timelines.
needed_evidence: Aggregate funding data from PitchBook, Crunchbase, and Fusion Industry Association reports.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: Government funding for fusion (e.g., via DOE, EURATOM) is growing at >10% CAGR, signaling strategic priority.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: Policy support reduces risk and may accelerate commercialization.
needed_evidence: Budget allocations from DOE Office of Science, EURATOM, and national fusion programs.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: At least 5 fusion startups will have demonstrated net energy gain (Q>1) by 2027.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: If true, technical feasibility is proven; if false, fundamental physics challenges remain.
needed_evidence: Announcements of Q>1 from private companies (e.g., TAE Technologies, Helion, Zap Energy).

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: Fusion will first be commercialized for marine propulsion or industrial heat, not grid electricity.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: Shifts market focus; grid electricity may be later.
needed_evidence: Company roadmaps targeting non-electric applications, e.g., Helion's data center plans.

hypotheses_to_test.hypothesis: China will lead in fusion patent filings and pilot plants by 2030, surpassing the US and EU.
hypotheses_to_test.decision_relevance: Geopolitical implications; may affect technology transfer and supply chains.
needed_evidence: Patent data from WIPO, China's CFETR timeline, and government statements.

market_sizing_logic.formula: Total addressable market (TAM) = Global electricity generation (kWh) × fusion share (%) × price ($/kWh)
known_limitations: Assumes fusion can compete on price; share is speculative.

market_sizing_logic.formula: Market size = Number of fusion plants × Average plant capacity (MW) × Capacity factor × Revenue per MWh
variables: Number of plants (based on announced projects and build rate)
Plant capacity (e.g., 500 MW per ARC reactor)
known_limitations: Few announced projects; build rate highly uncertain.

market_sizing_logic.formula: Market penetration = (Awareness × Interest × Trial × Adoption) of potential customers (utilities, industrials)
variables: Survey data on willingness to adopt fusion
known_limitations: Fusion awareness is low; survey data may be unreliable.

market_sizing_logic.formula: Value pool = Revenue from electricity sales + Carbon credits + Grid services + Hydrogen production
known_limitations: Carbon and hydrogen markets are nascent; assumptions needed.

market_sizing_logic.formula: Market size = Total fusion investment (public + private) × Investment-to-capacity multiplier ($/MW)
variables: Investment per MW (from company data, e.g., $7B for 500 MW)
known_limitations: Investment-to-capacity multiplier is highly uncertain; few data points.

charting_rule: Charts may use only evidence_ledger values, dates, source counts or same-unit comparable values tied to public URLs.
exhibit_narrative_rule: Every exhibit must be embedded in the section argument: prose sets up the management question before the exhibit and interprets the implication after it; never stack exhibits without prose.
